oracles.title: NullReferenceException in CoffeeMachineController.FinishBrew
oracles.description: Developer added GetComponent<AudioSource>().Play() in FinishBrew without null-check. No AudioSource exists on this GameObject, causing NullReferenceException every time brew completes.
oracles.trigger_condition: Complete coffee brewing (TryStartBrew → wait brewDuration → FinishBrew)
oracles.impact: Exception logged on every successful brew. SetCoffeeBrewed() is called first so state advances, but the exception indicates a crash-level bug.
oracles.fix: Add null-check: var audio = GetComponent<AudioSource>(); if (audio != null) audio.Play();

oracles.title: Coffee machine brews without cup in socket
oracles.description: TryStartBrew checks PowerOn but does NOT check cupSocket.HasCup. The coffee machine brews even when no cup is placed in the socket.
oracles.trigger_condition: Press brew button without placing Cup_Coffee in the CoffeeCupSocket
oracles.impact: Coffee brews into nothing, wasting the brew cycle. State coffeeBrewed becomes true without a cup present.
oracles.fix: Add guard: if (cupSocket != null && !cupSocket.HasCup) { Debug.LogWarning(...); return; }

oracles.title: Circuit breaker OFF does not reset PowerOn state
oracles.description: CircuitBreakerController.Toggle() calls SetPowerOn() when IsOn becomes true, but does NOT call SetPowerOff() when IsOn becomes false. ApartmentStateController.PowerOn stays true forever once set.
oracles.trigger_condition: Toggle circuit breaker ON, then toggle OFF again
oracles.impact: State inconsistency between physical breaker position (OFF) and logical power state (true). Downstream systems (TV, coffee machine, blinds) still see power as enabled.

oracles.title: Toaster timer not reset on bread re-insert
oracles.description: When bread is removed mid-toast (OnBreadRemoved sets IsToasting=false) and then re-inserted, _toastTimer is NOT reset to 0. Accumulated time from the previous attempt carries over, causing toast to complete instantly or prematurely.
oracles.trigger_condition: Insert bread → start toasting → remove bread before done → re-insert bread
oracles.impact: Skip the intended 3-second toast duration. If previous toast accumulated e.g. 2.9s, a re-insertion immediately completes the toast.

oracles.title: Window blind opens downward instead of upward (sign error)
oracles.description: WindowBlindController.Start() computes _openLocalPosition with NEGATIVE Y offset. The correct direction should be POSITIVE Y (up). Blind slides downward / into the floor instead of opening upward.
oracles.trigger_condition: Toggle window blind open (requires power from circuit breaker first)
oracles.impact: Blind visually slides downward into the floor geometry. Functionally the state says 'open' but the visual is wrong — window remains blocked.
oracles.fix: Change sign: new Vector3(0f, openDistance, 0f) instead of new Vector3(0f, -openDistance, 0f)

oracles.title: Faucet washes dishes without power — skips hot-water gate
oracles.description: TryWashDishes() checks only IsRunning but does NOT check PowerOn for hot water. Dishes can be washed with cold water, bypassing the hot-water heater requirement.
oracles.trigger_condition: Turn on faucet (Toggle) without circuit breaker power, then call TryWashDishes
oracles.impact: Critical gate bypass. Dishes can be washed without power, skipping the circuit breaker requirement. Downstream state advances incorrectly.
oracles.fix: Add power guard: if (ApartmentStateController.Instance != null && !ApartmentStateController.Instance.PowerOn) return;

oracles.title: Power enables with key pickup only — skips mailbox unlock step
oracles.description: SetPowerOn() checks hasMailKey instead of mailboxUnlocked. Player can enable power immediately after picking up the key, without actually inserting it into the mailbox lock socket. This skips step 2.
oracles.trigger_condition: Pick up Key_Mailbox → immediately toggle circuit breaker (without inserting key into mailbox lock socket)
oracles.impact: Gate skip: step 2 (mailboxUnlocked) can be bypassed. The downstream chain continues as if mailbox was opened. Power enables with just the key in hand.
oracles.fix: Restore precondition: if (!mailboxUnlocked) instead of if (!hasMailKey)

oracles.title: Fridge alarm indicator stays on after door closed
oracles.description: When fridge door stays open past alarmDelay, alarm indicator material turns red. On door close, _alarmActive flag is cleared but alarmRenderer.sharedMaterial is NOT restored to materialNormal. Indicator stays red.
oracles.trigger_condition: Open fridge door → wait > 5s for alarm → close door (indicator stays red)
oracles.impact: Visual-only: fridge door closes correctly but alarm indicator gives misleading red status. User may think fridge is still in alarm state.
oracles.fix: Restore material in close path: if (alarmRenderer != null) alarmRenderer.sharedMaterial = materialNormal;

oracles.title: TV CycleChannel calls SetTvNewsWatched before power check
oracles.description: CycleChannel() calls ApartmentStateController.SetTvNewsWatched(IsPowered) BEFORE checking if IsPowered is true. When TV is off, SetTvNewsWatched(false) is called, hitting the state controller's 'not powered' guard and producing a spurious FAIL warning.
oracles.trigger_condition: Call CycleChannel when the TV is NOT powered on
oracles.impact: Spurious ApartmentStateController FAIL log. No functional damage (guard prevents state change), but pollutes logs and confuses test analysis.
oracles.fix: Move SetTvNewsWatched call after the IsPowered guard, only when channel actually changes.

oracles.title: ResetAllState does not reset downstream controller flags
oracles.description: ApartmentStateController.ResetAllState() clears its 10 boolean flags but does not notify downstream controllers (CircuitBreakerController.IsOn, CoffeeMachineController.CurrentState, ToasterController.IsDone, etc.). After reset, routine state is fresh but controllers retain stale local state from previous run.
oracles.impact: State desync after reset. Controllers think steps are already completed. Second test run produces incorrect results if same scene instance is reused.
oracles.fix: Add reset events or iterate FindObjectsOfType to reset all controller flags.

state_oracles.label: routine_monotonic
state_oracles.description: Routine flags must be set in strict order (1→10). No flag should be true if its predecessor is false.
state_oracles.check: If coffeeBrewed=true then cupPlaced must be true; if breakfastServed=true then coffeeBrewed and toastMade must be true; etc.

state_oracles.label: completion_consistency
state_oracles.check: Both breakfastServed and tvNewsWatched must be true when routineComplete is reported.

state_oracles.label: power_state_sync
state_oracles.check: Both must be true or both false at any observation point.
